scads 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Quick Tutorial on scads

    Compute Single‑Cell Topic Enrichment Scores (c_i)

    get_cs2.Rd

    This function computes per‑cell enrichment scores \(c_i\) by combining fastTopics outputs (peak‑by‑topic probabilities and cell‑by‑topic loadings) with per‑topic LDSC enrichment (or heritability τ) estimates and optional GWAS variant counts per peak. Both first‑order (only uncertainty in LDSC enrichment) and full second‑order (ratio variance via delta‑method) approximations are supported.

    get_cs2(
  topic_res,
  ldsc_res_dir,
  trait,
  sumstats_dir = NULL,
  v_option = "one",
  weight_by = "enrichment",
  cs_approx = "first"
)

    Arguments

    topic_res

    A list returned by run_fastTopics, containing Pmat (J × K peak × topic probability matrix) and Lmat (I × K cell × topic loadings matrix).

    ldsc_res_dir

    Path to the directory where, for each topic k, the file k<k>_output/results/<trait>.results holds LDSC output with columns Enrichment and Enrichment_std_error.

    trait

    Character string; the trait name used to locate <trait>.results within each topic’s LDSC results folder.

    sumstats_dir

    Optional character: path to a folder of <trait>_sumstats.txt.gz summary‑stats files. Required if v_option="gwas".

    v_option

    Character, one of "one" (assume exactly one variant per peak) or "gwas" (count variants per peak from GWAS summary‑stats via sumstats_dir).

    weight_by

    Character, one of "enrichment" (use LDSC Enrichment estimates) or "tau" (use raw Prop._h2 τ estimates) as peak weights.

    cs_approx

    Character, one of "first" (delta‑method propagating only LDSC enrichment SE) or "second" (full ratio variance using EM, EN, Var(M), Var(N), Cov(M,N)).

    Value

    A list with

    cs

    Numeric vector of length I, the per‑cell scores \(c_i\).

    var_cs

    Numeric vector of the same length, the variance of \(c_i\).

    ldsc_res_table

    Data.frame of per‑topic LDSC results (enrichment & SE).

    cs_dat

    A list with M_iVector of the numerator \(M_i\) used in the score. N_iVector of the denominator \(N_i\). Var_MiThe variance of \(M_i\) under the chosen approximation.

    Contents
